05.title: Bank declined — usually credit limit
05.diagnosis: The customer's card issuer refused the charge. This is almost always a credit limit, a bank-side fraud flag, or the card being near expiry. It's a hard decline — retrying on the same card won't help.
05.historical: On this merchant, retrying with UPI succeeds 84% of the time within 2 minutes.
05.action: Offer a different method immediately — UPI first, NetBanking second.
05.customerCopy: "Your bank declined this charge. Want to try UPI instead? It's faster and free."

51.title: Insufficient funds
51.diagnosis: The customer's account doesn't have enough balance for this charge.
51.historical: Retrying later the same day fails 91% of the time. Retrying in 3+ days succeeds 34%.
51.action: Don't retry today. Schedule a 3-day follow-up email or let customer initiate.
51.customerCopy: "This charge couldn't go through. Try again later, or use UPI for a faster payment."

ach_timeout.title: ACH didn't respond in time
ach_timeout.diagnosis: The customer's bank took longer than 30 seconds to confirm the ACH debit. This is a bank-side latency issue, not a decline. Routing has already fallen back to SEPA Instant.
ach_timeout.historical: When ACH times out, SEPA Instant fallback succeeds 97% of the time.
ach_timeout.action: No action required — fallback handled it. Monitor if ACH timeout pattern grows.
ach_timeout.customerCopy: (Customer does not see this — resolved transparently.)

velocity_first_time_high_value.title: Velocity rule — first-time, high amount
velocity_first_time_high_value.diagnosis: New account (7 days old), attempting an amount 3× the merchant's median ticket. Matches a pattern that historically correlates with stolen-card testing.
velocity_first_time_high_value.historical: Across 47 merchants, 91% of attempts matching this pattern are confirmed fraudulent within 30 days.
velocity_first_time_high_value.action: Blocked is correct. If the customer reaches out, verify ID manually before allowing retry.
velocity_first_time_high_value.customerCopy: "We need to verify a few details before accepting this charge. Contact support@[merchant].com — we'll get this sorted quickly."

card_enumeration.title: Card enumeration — almost certainly a bot
card_enumeration.diagnosis: Four cards attempted from the same device in under 2 minutes. This is classic card-testing behavior — an attacker with a stolen card list checking which are still live.
card_enumeration.historical: 98% of enumeration patterns are malicious. The 2% legitimate cases are almost always developer testing.
card_enumeration.action: Blocked is correct. Consider a device-level block for 24 hours.
card_enumeration.customerCopy: (No customer communication — this is a bot, not a person.)

geo_restriction.title: Request from a restricted region
geo_restriction.diagnosis: Card issued in India, payment attempted from an IP in a country your merchant has blocked (or VPN detected).
geo_restriction.historical: This rule blocks 89 attempts per month platform-wide — 100% confirmed legitimate blocks (no false positives).
geo_restriction.action: Blocked is correct. If this is a real customer traveling, they should turn off VPN and retry.
geo_restriction.customerCopy: "We couldn't verify the location of this payment. If you're using a VPN, please disable it and try again."

default.title: No enrichment available for this event
default.diagnosis: This event doesn't match a known pattern. In production, Payze would describe the cause from live rules metadata.
